race_name: The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series

top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 98, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 98 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
73ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 98 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
7,260ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series. Train it until it feels boring.
7,260ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 98 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: March in Castellón de la Plana, Castellón Province, Spain — March average 18-20°C; ideal early-season conditions in Spain's second most mountainous province[1][2]. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series’s 98 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 98 miles of mixed terrain in Castellón de la Plana, Castellón Province, Spain.
